title: Black Tie

jokes.text: Yo' mama is so stupid, she thinks an iMac is a new hamburger at McDonald's
jokes.setup: Classic 'yo mama' joke structure
jokes.punchline: Wordplay on 'iMac' and 'Big Mac'
jokes.explanation: Standard yo mama joke with mild wordplay. Gets the job done but nothing special.

jokes.text: When I drop a load in the washing machine, it doesn't follow me around for a week
jokes.setup: What's the difference between yo mama and a washing machine?
jokes.punchline: Sexual innuendo about clingy behavior
jokes.explanation: Clever double entendre with good economy. More sophisticated than typical yo mama jokes.

jokes.text: That's what your sister asked me last night. Booyah!
jokes.setup: Lemon asks to speak with Jack alone
jokes.punchline: Sexual comeback playing off the request
jokes.explanation: Generic 'that's what she said' variant. Predictable but functional.

jokes.text: Prince Gerhardt is the last male descendant of the imperial house of Hapsburg. They ruled the Austro-Hungarian Empire, The Defenestration of Prague
jokes.setup: Jack explaining the prince's credentials
jokes.punchline: Casually mentioning 'Defenestration of Prague' as if it's a normal accomplishment
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jack character moment - showing off his knowledge while being completely tone-deaf about what's impressive vs. horrifying.

jokes.text: Theatre tech. I see.
jokes.setup: Jack asks what Liz studied in college
jokes.punchline: Jack's disappointed 'I see' reaction to theatre tech
jokes.explanation: Brilliant economy - Jack's entire worldview about class and education compressed into two words.

jokes.text: No. They're just really chapped.
jokes.setup: Jack compliments Liz on wearing lipstick
jokes.punchline: Reveals her 'lipstick' is just chapped lips
jokes.explanation: Perfect Liz moment - deflating Jack's attempt to see improvement with harsh reality.

jokes.text: That's what your mom said to me last night. Booyah.
jokes.setup: Liz says she'll do it but won't like it
jokes.punchline: Another sexual comeback with 'booyah'
jokes.explanation: Running gag of sexual comebacks. Repetitive but establishing a pattern.

jokes.text: [High-pitched voice] Elmo wants you to aim your pee-pee at the potty. No, not at Mommy. At the potty.
jokes.setup: Pete on phone doing voice work
jokes.punchline: Reveals he's doing Elmo voice for potty training gone wrong
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character moment - Pete's humiliation made worse by having to do it in Elmo voice. Perfect cringe comedy.

jokes.text: Did Mommy have some wine before she called Elmo?
jokes.setup: Pete continuing as Elmo on phone
jokes.punchline: Elmo asking about drinking problem
jokes.explanation: Dark genius - Elmo voice delivering family dysfunction commentary. Perfect character integration and subversive humor.

jokes.text: Pete, there are two types of women in this world... one who gives you strength and one who takes strength from you, like Delilah took strength from Samson in that movie.
jokes.setup: Tracy offering relationship advice
jokes.punchline: 'in that movie' - reducing biblical story to casual movie reference
jokes.explanation: Great Tracy moment - profound-sounding advice undermined by his casual pop culture reference frame.

jokes.text: Pete, Pete, Pete, Pete. Where's your spinal cord, son?
jokes.setup: Pete explaining his wife situation
jokes.punchline: Tracy's blunt assessment of Pete's backbone
jokes.explanation: Good Tracy bluntness, anatomical metaphor for courage

jokes.text: That's why eight times a week I go to the strip club. It gives me energy, which I bring back to her.
jokes.setup: Tracy explaining his marriage philosophy
jokes.punchline: Eight times a week strip club visits as relationship maintenance
jokes.explanation: Perfect Tracy logic - completely sincere justification for absurd behavior. 'Eight times a week' is brilliant detail.

jokes.text: You mean like a dog birthday party?
jokes.setup: Liz mentions going to a prince's birthday party
jokes.punchline: Jenna assumes it's a dog party
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Jenna's limited worldview where dog parties are more plausible than actual royalty.

jokes.text: No, Liz. Cinderella is blond. You can be Snow White and party with the little people.
jokes.setup: Liz suggesting she could be Cinderella
jokes.punchline: Jenna's casual cruelty about Liz's appearance and social status
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly cruel Jenna moment. Perfect character integration - she delivers devastating insults as helpful suggestions.

jokes.text: Ow! That really hurt my hand! Dag! Who does that?!
jokes.setup: Jack gives Liz jewelry
jokes.punchline: Jenna's over-the-top reaction to slapping the jewelry
jokes.explanation: Physical comedy moment with Jenna's typical self-centered reaction to pain.

jokes.text: This is definitely a date. No. Is it?
jokes.setup: Jenna declaring it's a date after seeing jewelry
jokes.punchline: Liz's uncertainty despite evidence
jokes.explanation: Liz's romantic obliviousness played for gentle comedy

jokes.text: Lemon, I date socialites and models and actresses, Liz Hurley... in the '90s.
jokes.setup: Jack explaining his dating standards
jokes.punchline: The '90s qualifier undermining his brag
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jack pomposity undercut by honest detail. The pause before '...in the '90s' is comedy gold.

jokes.text: You showed the security guy your boobs, didn't you? Just one. It's not the White House.
jokes.setup: Liz asking how Jenna got in
jokes.punchline: Jenna's casual scaling of security requirements
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation structure and perfect Jenna logic - security clearance by breast count.

jokes.text: Austria. Yeah. That's what I said.
jokes.setup: Jenna talking about being queen of Australia
jokes.punchline: Doubling down on her geography mistake
jokes.explanation: Classic Jenna - never admitting mistakes. Good character moment but predictable.

jokes.text: Prince Gerhardt Messerschmitt Rammstein Von Hap!
jokes.setup: Formal royal announcement
jokes.punchline: Names including 'Messerschmitt' (Nazi plane) and 'Rammstein' (metal band)
jokes.explanation: Clever historical/pop culture mashup in royal names. Rewatchable for hidden references.

jokes.text: Who's Snow White now?
jokes.setup: Seeing the very pale, sickly prince
jokes.punchline: Referring back to her earlier Snow White insult to Liz
jokes.explanation: Excellent callback with perfect economy. Jenna's competitiveness even extends to fairy tale comparisons.

jokes.text: Most people in his situation would be angry with their family for the centuries of inbreeding, but not Gerhardt. He's too busy trying to stave off infection.
jokes.setup: Jack praising Gerhardt's positive attitude
jokes.punchline: Revealing the horrifying medical reality behind the attitude
jokes.explanation: Masterful dark comedy. Jack's complete tone-deafness delivering horrifying information as social commentary. Perfect structure and character integration.

jokes.text: And surprisingly age-appropriate.
jokes.setup: Liz commenting on Jack's ex-wife being gorgeous
jokes.punchline: Surprise that Jack would date someone age-appropriate
jokes.explanation: Good observational humor about Jack's typical dating patterns. Efficient character insight.

jokes.text: And unlike the rest of Jack's girlfriends, I have all my original parts.
jokes.setup: Liz playing along as Jack's girlfriend
jokes.punchline: Implying his usual dates have had plastic surgery
jokes.explanation: Good Liz comeback with surgical/authenticity humor

jokes.text: It was part of the Big Brother Little Sister program.
jokes.setup: Asked how he and Liz met
jokes.punchline: Implying a mentor relationship rather than romantic
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jack move - solving social awkwardness by creating more awkwardness. Great misdirection structure.

jokes.text: She's much sharper than the last girl you had. What was her name? Beyoncé.
jokes.setup: Complimenting Liz's intelligence
jokes.punchline: Casual namedrop of dating Beyoncé
jokes.explanation: Good escalation revealing Jack's absurd dating history. Celebrity name-drop works because it's so casual.

jokes.text: And unlike the rest of Jack's girlfriends, I have all my original parts.
jokes.setup: Continuing the girlfriend comparison
jokes.punchline: Implying Jack's other girlfriends are heavily surgically modified
jokes.explanation: Good Liz comeback - dark humor about beauty standards while defending herself.

jokes.text: His highness is attempting to wink at you.
jokes.setup: Aide explaining the prince's romantic interest
jokes.punchline: The prince is so medically compromised he can't properly wink
jokes.explanation: Perfect visual gag setup. The formal announcement of a failed wink is comedy gold.

jokes.text: I'm an actress, Liz. It would be my greatest role of all time.
jokes.setup: Discussing marrying the sickly prince for money
jokes.punchline: Framing gold-digging as an acting challenge
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jenna - turning mercenary calculation into artistic achievement. Brilliant character integration.

jokes.text: You're not even listening, are you? Poop. Monkey butt.
jokes.setup: Liz realizing Jenna isn't listening to advice
jokes.punchline: Testing with nonsense words
jokes.explanation: Classic sitcom bit but executed well. Good character moment showing Liz's practical approach to dealing with Jenna.

jokes.text: He saved my life when we was in Desert Storm. Our tank broke down. He killed 14 Germans so we could get the hell out of there.
jokes.setup: Tracy explaining Pete's military background
jokes.punchline: Completely anachronistic story placing Germans in Desert Storm
jokes.explanation: Perfect Tracy moment - completely sincere delivery of historically impossible story. Great character integration.

jokes.text: Sexually... she wanted it four or five times a day, always standing up. Standing up. What? How does that even work?
jokes.setup: Jack oversharing about his ex-wife
jokes.punchline: Liz's innocent confusion about the logistics
jokes.explanation: Good cringe comedy with Liz's naivety playing against Jack's oversharing. Character-appropriate awkwardness.

jokes.text: Sadly, because my body does not produce joint fluid, I cannot.
jokes.setup: Jenna asking if he wants to dance
jokes.punchline: Medical impossibility delivered with courtly politeness
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark comedy - horrifying medical condition delivered with perfect courtly manners. Original and character-specific.

jokes.text: But I would enjoy watching you dance for me.
jokes.setup: Cannot dance himself
jokes.punchline: Requests private performance like she's entertainment
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment showing the prince's objectification through aristocratic politeness.

jokes.text: Jazz! Tap! Jitterbug! Charleston! Interpretive! Twirl! Twirl again! Keep twirling!
jokes.setup: Jenna performing for the prince
jokes.punchline: Increasingly desperate dance moves culminating in endless twirling
jokes.explanation: Great escalation structure showing Jenna's desperation. Visual comedy that builds momentum effectively.

jokes.text: Everyone thinks I'm 5'5", but I'm only 5'4".
jokes.setup: Prince sharing personal information
jokes.punchline: Revealing his height deception of one inch
jokes.explanation: Perfect absurdist moment - the smallest possible deception treated as major confession. Brilliant economy.

jokes.text: Do you remember the movie "Footloose," where those evil kids won in the end?
jokes.setup: Kenneth giving moral guidance about dancing
jokes.punchline: Completely backwards interpretation of Footloose
jokes.explanation: Perfect Kenneth moment - his warped moral universe where dancing is evil. Brilliant character-specific absurdism.

jokes.text: Finally old enough to rent a car.
jokes.setup: Prince turning 25
jokes.punchline: Major life milestone is car rental eligibility
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection - royal heir excited about mundane middle-class milestone. Efficient character moment.

jokes.text: I've never done it in a men's bathroom before. Well, in this building.
jokes.setup: Woman seducing Pete in bathroom
jokes.punchline: Implying she's done it in other bathrooms in the building
jokes.explanation: Good escalation revealing character's extensive building experience

jokes.text: Be a Samson! You're a better man than this. But she's so smooth.
jokes.setup: Tracy encouraging Pete to resist temptation
jokes.punchline: Pete's weak justification for giving in
jokes.explanation: Callback to Samson reference with Pete's pathetic rationalization

jokes.text: My wife is not a Delilah, Tracy.
jokes.setup: Pete rejecting temptation
jokes.punchline: References Tracy's earlier biblical relationship advice
jokes.explanation: Good callback showing Pete absorbed Tracy's advice. Shows character growth.

jokes.text: She's so smooth.
jokes.setup: Pete explaining attraction to other woman
jokes.punchline: Shallow physical attribute as major selling point
jokes.explanation: Decent character moment showing Pete's simplistic thinking, but fairly predictable.

jokes.text: Who's that tickling my feet? I'm gonna kick you in your face. I know that.
jokes.setup: Tracy in bathroom stall
jokes.punchline: Threatening violence over foot tickling
jokes.explanation: Basic bathroom humor with Tracy's aggressive response

jokes.text: A little late-in-life baby he can parade around Nantucket.
jokes.setup: Bianca predicting Jack's future with Liz
jokes.punchline: Reducing potential child to status symbol prop
jokes.explanation: Good character insight into upper-class values. Dark humor about children as accessories.

jokes.text: And that makes me want to sit on a knife.
jokes.setup: Explaining her jealousy of Liz
jokes.punchline: Violent imagery for emotional response
jokes.explanation: Good dark humor showing Bianca's elegant exterior hiding violent impulses.

jokes.text: [Both screaming]
jokes.setup: Liz announces fake engagement to make Bianca jealous
jokes.punchline: Immediate violent physical altercation
jokes.explanation: Good escalation to physical comedy. Predictable but well-executed catfight moment.

jokes.text: I know we have just met, but I think I love you. Do you love me? Mm... hmm.
jokes.setup: Gerhardt confessing love after brief meeting
jokes.punchline: Jenna's noncommittal response to desperate question
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment showing both characters' desperation. Jenna's hesitant response is perfect.

jokes.text: I think you just lost an eyebrow.
jokes.setup: Prince celebrating his newfound love
jokes.punchline: Physical deterioration from excitement
jokes.explanation: Great visual gag callback to the prince's medical condition. Dark and original.

jokes.text: Who let him drink champagne? He cannot metabolize the grapes!
jokes.setup: Prince's condition worsening
jokes.punchline: Specific medical impossibility delivered with panic
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation with specific absurd medical detail. Great panic delivery.

jokes.text: Meine Damen und Herren! The Hapsburg line... has ended.
jokes.setup: Formal announcement after prince's death
jokes.punchline: Grandiose delivery of dynastic extinction
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly dramatic announcement of historical significance

jokes.text: You can pick up your gift bags at the coat check.
jokes.setup: Announcement of dynasty ending
jokes.punchline: Immediate return to party logistics
jokes.explanation: Perfect comedy juxtaposition - historical tragedy to party logistics

jokes.text: The Hapsburg line... has ended. You can pick up your gift bags at the coat check.
jokes.setup: Announcing the prince's death and end of royal bloodline
jokes.punchline: Immediately segueing to gift bag logistics
jokes.explanation: Masterpiece of dark comedy. Perfect juxtaposition of historical tragedy with party planning banality. Incredible economy and structure.

jokes.text: When Gerhardt was born, the doctor told his mother and cousin he would either live for 15 minutes or for 100 years. Boy, he proved them all wrong.
jokes.setup: Seemingly touching story about medical predictions
jokes.punchline: He lived exactly 25 years - the worst possible outcome
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark misdirection. Sets up expectation of triumph but reveals the mathematical worst case. Perfect Jack tone-deafness.

jokes.text: I'm 12 years younger than you. A woman your age, then.
jokes.setup: Jack thanking Liz for showing him he could date a woman his age
jokes.punchline: Jack doubling down on the insult after correction
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jack moment - never backing down from offensive statements. Great economy and character consistency.

jokes.text: I had "lunch" with Martha Stewart and "dinner" with her daughter, Alexis. Gross.
jokes.setup: Jack listing his famous conquests
jokes.punchline: Air quotes implying sexual encounters with mother and daughter, Liz's horrified reaction
jokes.explanation: Perfect ending - Jack's casual revelation of something horrifying, with Liz's reaction speaking for the audience. Great character moment.

tmdb_overview: Liz wonders if she's actually on a date with Jack as she accompanies him to a birthday celebration for a foreign prince. While at the ball, Liz sees a different side to Jack as he unexpectedly runs in to his ex-wife. Back at 30 Rock, Tracy tempts Pete with a booze- and babe-filled party in the office, while Kenneth tries to keep Pete on the straight and narrow.
insight: Mid-season slump hits 30 Rock: 77 score on character comedy alone, no standout zingers.
